title: onoclea struthiopteris var. pensylvanica
intro: Onoclea struthiopteris var. pensylvanica, also known as the Pennsylvania ostrich fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Osmundaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Onoclea struthiopteris var. pensylvanica include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract. In herbal preparations, the fronds of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
